title: WIP: test docker-compose
body:
git-fetch a set of docker-compose examples

Add a mechanism for 'curl'ing to verify that service is up.

For each docker-compose directory:
  1) test that it comes up
  2) use 'curl' to confirm that the service is up
  3) bring it back down

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@baude this isn't working, and I'm at a complete loss to understand why. Basically, the `curl` step is failing. If I put a pause in the script (included in this WIP), I have all the time in the world to run `lsof -i -n -P | grep 3000` and confirm that a podman process is listening on `*:3000`; but no variation of `curl http://127.0.0.1:3000/` will give me any results. I've tried removing the `--cni-config-dir` option, no difference. I'm stepping away from it for the rest of today. Maybe you'll have better luck.

Progress so far: nil. Absolutely no clue. My findings, in case they're helpful:
* it has to do with `--root`; the `--cni-config-dir` option is irrelevant, as is pre-copying `87-podman*conflist` into the tmp cni dir.
* it only happens with docker-compose; I have been unable to manually create a container, using `sudo ./bin/podman`, that recreates this behavior
* if I remove the `--root` and `--runroot` from the script, it works as expected. The port responds on localhost.

Could this be a weird interaction between `--root` and APIv2?
@edsantiago @baude Is this still valid, or has another version of this been merged?
OOPS! Sorry, this was just a launching board for @baude to use in his tests (Which are already merged). Closing.
